examples.title: Pseudocode: CPU Scheduling Algorithms
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin CPU scheduling algorithms with waiting and turnaround time calculations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FCFS_Scheduling
INPUT: List of processes with arrival time and burst time
OUTPUT: Gantt chart, waiting times, turnaround times

BEGIN
 SORT processes by arrival time (if same, by process ID)
 current_time = 0
 total_waiting_time = 0
 total_turnaround_time = 0
 
 FOR each process in ready_queue DO
 IF current_time < process.arrival_time THEN
 current_time = process.arrival_time
 END IF
 
 process.start_time = current_time
 process.completion_time = current_time + process.burst_time
 process.turnaround_time = process.completion_time - process.arrival_time
 process.waiting_time = process.turnaround_time - process.burst_time
 
 current_time = process.completion_time
 total_waiting_time = total_waiting_time + process.waiting_time
 total_turnaround_time = total_turnaround_time + process.turnaround_time
 
 PRINT "Process " + process.id + ": Start=" + start_time + 
 " Complete=" + completion_time + " Wait=" + waiting_time
 END FOR
 
 PRINT "Average Waiting Time: " + (total_waiting_time / num_processes)
 PRINT "Average Turnaround Time: " + (total_turnaround_time / num_processes)
END

ALGORITHM SJF_Scheduling
INPUT: List of processes with arrival time and burst time
OUTPUT: Gantt chart with optimal waiting times

BEGIN
 current_time = 0
 completed = 0
 WHILE completed < num_processes DO
 ready_queue = []
 FOR each process DO
 IF process.arrival_time <= current_time AND NOT process.completed THEN
 ADD process to ready_queue
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF ready_queue is empty THEN
 current_time = current_time + 1
 CONTINUE
 END IF
 
 SELECT process with shortest burst_time from ready_queue
 process.start_time = current_time
 process.completion_time = current_time + process.burst_time
 process.turnaround_time = process.completion_time - process.arrival_time
 process.waiting_time = process.turnaround_time - process.burst_time
 
 current_time = process.completion_time
 process.completed = TRUE
 completed = completed + 1
 END WHILE
END

ALGORITHM RoundRobin_Scheduling
INPUT: List of processes, time_quantum
OUTPUT: Gantt chart with context switching

BEGIN
 current_time = 0
 ready_queue = FIFO queue of processes that have arrived
 remaining_burst[] = copy of all burst times
 
 WHILE any process has remaining_burst > 0 DO
 IF ready_queue is empty THEN
 current_time = find next arrival time
 CONTINUE
 END IF
 
 process = DEQUEUE from ready_queue
 time_slice = MIN(time_quantum, remaining_burst[process])
 
 remaining_burst[process] = remaining_burst[process] - time_slice
 current_time = current_time + time_slice
 
 FOR each other_process DO
 IF other_process has arrived AND other_process != process THEN
 ADD to ready_queue if not already waiting
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF remaining_burst[process] > 0 THEN
 ADD process back to ready_queue
 ELSE
 process.completion_time = current_time
 process.turnaround_time = completion_time - arrival_time
 process.waiting_time = turnaround_time - original_burst
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates three fundamental CPU scheduling algorithms. FCFS processes arrive in order of their arrival time. SJF selects the process with the shortest burst time from the ready queue, providing optimal average waiting time. Round Robin uses time-slicing where each process gets a quantum of CPU time in a circular fashion, making it suitable for time-sharing systems.

examples.title: Python: CPU Scheduling Algorithms Implementation
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin with Gantt chart generation and performance metrics
examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
CPU Scheduling Algorithms Implementation
Implements FCFS, SJF (Non-preemptive), and Round Robin
"""

class Process:
 def __init__(self, pid, arrival_time, burst_time, priority=None):
 self.pid = pid
 self.arrival_time = arrival_time
 self.burst_time = burst_time
 self.priority = priority
 self.remaining_time = burst_time
 self.completion_time = 0
 self.turnaround_time = 0
 self.waiting_time = 0
 self.start_time = -1

def fcfs_scheduling(processes):
 """First-Come, First-Served Scheduling"""
 # Sort by arrival time, then by PID
 sorted_processes = sorted(processes, key=lambda p: (p.arrival_time, p.pid))
 
 current_time = 0
 gantt_chart = []
 
 for process in sorted_processes:
 if current_time < process.arrival_time:
 current_time = process.arrival_time
 
 process.start_time = current_time
 gantt_chart.append((process.pid, current_time, current_time + process.burst_time))
 
 process.completion_time = current_time + process.burst_time
 process.turnaround_time = process.completion_time - process.arrival_time
 process.waiting_time = process.turnaround_time - process.burst_time
 
 current_time = process.completion_time
 
 return sorted_processes, gantt_chart

def sjf_scheduling(processes):
 """Shortest Job First (Non-preemptive) Scheduling"""
 # Create copies to avoid modifying original
 proc_list = [Process(p.pid, p.arrival_time, p.burst_time, p.priority) for p in processes]
 
 current_time = 0
 completed = 0
 gantt_chart = []
 n = len(proc_list)
 
 while completed < n:
 # Find all processes that have arrived and not completed
 ready_queue = [p for p in proc_list if p.arrival_time <= current_time and p.completion_time == 0]
 
 if not ready_queue:
 # No process ready, find next arrival
 next_arrival = min(p.arrival_time for p in proc_list if p.completion_time == 0)
 current_time = next_arrival
 continue
 
 # Select process with shortest burst time
 ready_queue.sort(key=lambda p: (p.burst_time, p.arrival_time))
 process = ready_queue[0]
 
 process.start_time = current_time
 gantt_chart.append((process.pid, current_time, current_time + process.burst_time))
 
 process.completion_time = current_time + process.burst_time
 process.turnaround_time = process.completion_time - process.arrival_time
 process.waiting_time = process.turnaround_time - process.burst_time
 
 current_time = process.completion_time
 completed += 1
 
 return proc_list, gantt_chart

def round_robin_scheduling(processes, time_quantum):
 """Round Robin Scheduling"""
 proc_list = [Process(p.pid, p.arrival_time, p.burst_time, p.priority) for p in processes]
 
 from collections import deque
 
 current_time = 0
 ready_queue = deque()
 gantt_chart = []
 n = len(proc_list)
 completed = 0
 
 # Initialize: add processes that arrive at time 0
 for p in proc_list:
 if p.arrival_time == 0:
 ready_queue.append(p)
 
 completed_pids = set()
 
 while completed < n:
 if not ready_queue:
 # Jump to next arrival
 next_arrival = min(p.arrival_time for p in proc_list if p.pid not in completed_pids)
 current_time = next_arrival
 for p in proc_list:
 if p.arrival_time <= current_time and p.pid not in completed_pids:
 ready_queue.append(p)
 continue
 
 process = ready_queue.popleft()
 
 if process.start_time == -1:
 process.start_time = current_time
 
 # Execute for time quantum or remaining time
 exec_time = min(time_quantum, process.remaining_time)
 gantt_chart.append((process.pid, current_time, current_time + exec_time))
 
 process.remaining_time -= exec_time
 current_time += exec_time
 
 # Add newly arrived processes to queue
 for p in proc_list:
 if (p.arrival_time <= current_time and 
 p.pid not in completed_pids and 
 p != process and p not in ready_queue):
 ready_queue.append(p)
 
 if process.remaining_time > 0:
 ready_queue.append(process)
 else:
 process.completion_time = current_time
 process.turnaround_time = process.completion_time - process.arrival_time
 process.waiting_time = process.turnaround_time - process.burst_time
 completed_pids.add(process.pid)
 completed += 1
 
 return proc_list, gantt_chart

def print_results(processes, algorithm_name, gantt_chart):
 """Print scheduling results"""
 print(f"\n{'='*60}")
 print(f"{algorithm_name}")
 print(f"{'='*60}")
 
 print("\nGantt Chart:")
 print("|", end="")
 for pid, start, end in gantt_chart:
 duration = end - start
 bar = f" P{pid} " * (duration // 2 + 1)
 print(bar[:duration*2], end="")
 print("|", end="")
 print()
 
 # Timeline
 print("Time:", end=" ")
 time_points = [0]
 for _, start, end in gantt_chart:
 time_points.extend([start, end])
 for t in sorted(set(time_points)):
 print(f"{t:4}", end="")
 print()
 
 print("\nProcess Details:")
 print(f"{'PID':<6}{'AT':<6}{'BT':<6}{'CT':<6}{'TAT':<6}{'WT':<6}")
 print("-" * 34)
 
 total_wt = 0
 total_tat = 0
 for p in sorted(processes, key=lambda x: x.pid):
 print(f"{p.pid:<6}{p.arrival_time:<6}{p.burst_time:<6}{p.completion_time:<6}{p.turnaround_time:<6}{p.waiting_time:<6}")
 total_wt += p.waiting_time
 total_tat += p.turnaround_time
 
 n = len(processes)
 print(f"\nAverage Waiting Time: {total_wt/n:.2f}")
 print(f"Average Turnaround Time: {total_tat/n:.2f}")

# Main execution
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Sample processes: PID, Arrival Time, Burst Time
 processes_data = [
 (1, 0, 24),
 (2, 1, 3),
 (3, 2, 3)
 ]
 
 processes = [Process(pid, at, bt) for pid, at, bt in processes_data]
 
 # Test FCFS
 fcfs_procs, fcfs_gantt = fcfs_scheduling(processes)
 print_results(fcfs_procs, "FCFS Scheduling", fcfs_gantt)
 
 # Test SJF
 sjf_procs, sjf_gantt = sjf_scheduling(processes)
 print_results(sjf_procs, "SJF Scheduling", sjf_gantt)
 
 # Test Round Robin (quantum = 4)
 rr_procs, rr_gantt = round_robin_scheduling(processes, 4)
 print_results(rr_procs, "Round Robin (Quantum=4)", rr_gantt)
examples.explanation: This Python implementation provides complete scheduling algorithm functions. FCFS uses a simple sorted queue approach. SJF finds the shortest job among arrived processes. Round Robin uses a deque for the ready queue and time-slicing. Each function calculates completion time, turnaround time, and waiting time, then prints a Gantt chart representation.
examples.timeComplexity: FCFS: O(n log n), SJF: O(n²), Round Robin: O(n × (burst/quantum))

examples.title: C: CPU Scheduling Algorithms Implementation
examples.description: Complete C implementation of FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin with Gantt chart visualization and performance metrics
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates three major CPU scheduling algorithms. FCFS sorts by arrival time and executes in order. SJF selects the shortest burst from arrived processes. Round Robin uses a circular queue with time-slicing. Each algorithm calculates completion time, turnaround time, and waiting time, then displays results in a formatted table with average metrics.
examples.timeComplexity: FCFS: O(n log n), SJF: O(n²), Round Robin: O(n × (burst/quantum))
